title: prostanthera centralis
intro: Prostanthera centralis, also known as the Central Mint Bush, is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Prostanthera centralis include relief from digestive issues, colds, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. Prostanthera centralis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy due to its minty fragrance.
